title: Angkor Wat—A Transcultural History of Heritage Volume 1: Angkor in France. From Plaster Casts to Exhibition Pavilions. Volume 2: Angkor in Cambodia. From Jungle Find to Global Icon, by Michael Falser
highlights: Falser exposes how and why Mouhot, by the 1860s, formulated his "architectural hymn" for Angkor through a unique blend of French colonialist and missionary rhetoric.  ...    monograph discusses the multiple lives of Angkor Wat over a 150-year-long period from the 1860s to the 2010s. title: “Angkor Wat: A Transcultural History of Heritage” by Michael Falser
highlights: is not only about how the history of Angkor Wat went hand-in-hand with French intervention in Cambodia, but also a critical reflection on the practices of a European-derived concept of (cultural)  ...    Falser critically deals with Angkor Wat as a product of “transcultural entanglement”: its history since the French colonization in Indochina is layered by transcultural encounters between the actors involved, from French  ...    until 1922. Furthermore, as an archaeological site, Angkor Wat was promoted and claimed as a French cultural possession. He convincingly describes the deliberate and complex strategy through which the French appropriated Angkor Wat culturally, mentally and materially. The French involvement at Angkor, indeed, was only interrupted when the Khmer  ...
